bird.name: Spotless crake
bird.summary: The spotless crake (Zapornia tabuensis) is a species of bird in the rail family, Rallidae. It is widely distributed species occurring from…

locations.name: 查塔姆群岛
locations.summary: 查塔姆群岛（莫里奥里语：Rekohu；毛利語：Wharekauri；英語：Chatham Islands）是新西兰的一个群岛，由40千米范围内的约10个大小岛屿组成。这些位于新西兰东南方800多公里远的岛屿，于1982年正式归属新西兰管辖…

locations.name: 新西兰
locations.summary: 新西兰（英語：New Zealand）又称奥特亚罗瓦（毛利語：Aotearoa，直译：「长白云之乡」），位於太平洋西南部的主權島國，是英联邦新西蘭王國的主體部分，首都為惠灵顿，最大的城市為奧克蘭。紐西蘭主要由兩大島嶼組成：北島（Te Ik…

locations.name: 南島 (新西蘭)
locations.summary: 南島（英語：South Island；毛利語：Te Wai Pounamu）是组成紐西蘭的兩個主要岛屿之一，与北島被库克海峡隔断。新西兰首都惠灵顿即在南岛对岸，福沃海峡在南面，使之与斯图尔特岛分开。西面为塔斯曼海，东面为南太平洋。該島的毛…

relations.subject: Spotless crake
relations.evidence: Fossils of the spotless crake from the Holocene period have also been found on the mainland, as well as the Chatham Islands. Because the spotless crake is rarely seen, it is hard to determine what the population size is, so currently population numbers are unknown in New Zealand. It is believed that the reason spotless crake are rarely found in the South Island, New Zealand is due to the more dominant rail the marsh crake pushing the spotless crake out of suitable habitat, as the natural habitat of the two species are very similar

relations.object_summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

relations.subject: Spotless crake
relations.evidence: Another reason may be that spotless crakes are not as tolerant of the colder climates of the South Island, being mainly found in warmer coastal localities. Spotless crakes are freshwater wetland birds
relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

relations.subject: Spotless crake
relations.evidence: Their preferred habitat is wetland and swamp areas that contain dense vegetation in which to build their nests from. They can be found foraging on open muddy areas near dense vegetation, but when disturbed they retreat back into the vegetation
relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

relations.subject: Spotless crake
relations.evidence: Their preferred habitat is wetland and swamp areas that contain dense vegetation in which to build their nests from. They can be found foraging on open muddy areas near dense vegetation, but when disturbed they retreat back into the vegetation
relations.object_summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

relations.subject: Spotless crake
relations.evidence: On some small offshore islands, where wetlands are sparse, they have been known to live and forage in dry forest. Although raupo tends to be the preferred habitat for spotless crake, they have also been found in swamp areas composed of flax (Phormium tenax), tussock sedge (Carex secta), and cabbage tree (Cordyline australis). If habitat is not ideal, they are also able to migrate locally, which other subspecies do elsewhere
relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

relations.subject: Spotless crake
relations.evidence: Water pollution and degradation of wetlands due to grazing are also major threats. thumbtime=10|300px|left|SE Queensland, Australia
relations.object_summary: 农药、工业污染与水体污染影响食物链安全。
